summary: Iteration 102 — Verified the main agent's fix for 'Print/Export Drilling Protocol not working on native (iOS)'. Two-part review: (1) CODE REVIEW of native branch in /app/frontend/components/CaseImplantPlanning.tsx — PASSED. (2) WEB REGRESSION for nurse role at /procedures/69cf9bdfdafb502718057bcd — PASSED. Print POST /api/drilling-protocols/export-pdf returned 200 application/pdf, no 'Print failed'/'Export failed' Alerts, no JS pageerror. No fix applied.

checks_passed: printDrillingProtocolPdf (L75-106): Platform.OS === 'web' uses Blob + iframe (DOM); else path uses fetchDrillingPdfToCacheNative → Print.printAsync({uri}). No DOM APIs in native branch.

exportDrillingProtocolPdf (L108-139): Platform.OS === 'web' uses Blob + anchor.download; else path uses fetchDrillingPdfToCacheNative → Sharing.shareAsync(uri) with fallback to WebBrowser.openBrowserAsync. No DOM APIs in native branch.

fetchDrillingPdfToCacheNative (L58-73): POSTs with responseType 'arraybuffer', dynamic-imports `buffer` (Buffer.from(res.data).toString('base64')), dynamic-imports 'expo-file-system/legacy', writes to FileSystem.cacheDirectory with encoding: FileSystem.EncodingType.Base64. Matches consentPdf.ts iter97 pattern exactly.
Dynamic imports (buffer, expo-file-system/legacy, expo-print, expo-sharing, expo-web-browser) keep native modules off the web JS bundle.
Backend endpoint returns StreamingResponse of PDF bytes — axios `responseType: arraybuffer` reads this correctly on React Native (iter101 confirmed 200 application/pdf).

web_regression_repro_steps: 3. Scroll to Implant Planning section — MIS-Lance+ D:3.75mm L:11.5mm D2 position 36 card renders with 'Drilling Protocol' button visible (nurse non-canEdit branch).

4. Tap 'Drilling Protocol' → inline drill sequence renders (5 steps: Marking 1.9mm → Pilot 2.4mm → 3.1mm → Final 3.65mm → MIS LANCE+ Implant 3.75mm). 'Print / Export Drilling Protocol PDF' grey button renders at bottom.
5. Tap the Print/Export trigger → blue-bordered ExportPrintMenu popover opens with two options.
6. Tap 'Print drilling protocol' → POST /api/drilling-protocols/export-pdf → 200 application/pdf. No UI error. No 'Print failed' Alert.

network_capture.export_post: Not re-captured in iter102 automation (popover timing), but iter101 recorded 200 application/pdf for the same endpoint with identical payload. Web export code path is structurally identical to Print (same fetchDrillingPdfBlob helper).

action_items: NONE required. Native fix is structurally correct and web regression passes. Main agent can self-confirm via preview for web; native (iOS/Android) iOS flow cannot be exercised in this harness but the code uses the proven consent PDF pattern.
critical_code_review_comments: L79, L113 (web path): URL.revokeObjectURL(url) for export is called immediately after a.click() which in some browsers cancels the download before the blob URL is fetched. Consider setTimeout(()=>URL.revokeObjectURL(url), 15000) (same pattern as consentPdf.ts L44). Low priority — worked in iter101.
L70 catch inside fetchDrillingPdfToCacheNative logs err then returns null; caller prints 'Could not prepare PDF'. Good. Consider surfacing err.message to the Alert for easier on-device RCA.

L47 buildDrillingPdfBody: length defaults to 0 if falsy; backend accepts but the resulting PDF will say 'L:0mm'. Since plan.length is required in the UI flow this is benign, but consider `payload.implant.length ?? null` semantics if backend ever distinguishes.
Type safety: ImplantPlanItem does not declare 'tooth?: string' — plan.tooth is accessed at L736, L740 via fallback ''. Pre-existing from iter101 feedback; not blocking.

success_rate.frontend: 100% — Web Print confirmed 200 PDF; native code path verified DOM-free and mirrors consentPdf.ts proven pattern

context_for_next_testing_agent: iter102 confirms both (a) code-review of native branch (Platform.OS !== 'web' uses only fetchDrillingPdfToCacheNative → expo-print/expo-sharing; no DOM APIs) and (b) web regression (Print 200 application/pdf; no Alert). iOS/Android Expo Go build cannot be tested in this harness — main agent must ask the user to verify on their iPhone. If user still reports failure on native, request: exact error text from the iOS Alert dialog, console log from Expo dev tools, and confirm the bundle was reloaded after the edit. The fix mirrors consentPdf.ts line-for-line which was proven working in iter97.
